title: Frontispiece
content:
And 'mid the awful stillness
        Of their grave,
    The forest oaks have flourished 
        And the breath
    Of years hath swept their races,
        Wave on wave,
    As ages fainted
        On the shores of death.
    The tumbling cliff perchance
        Hath thundered deep,
    Like a rough note
        Of music in the song
    Of centuries, and the whirlwind's
        Crushing sweep,
    Hath ploughed the forest
        With its furrows strong.
meaning:
"Frontispiece" is a quintessential example of Samuel Griswold Goodrich's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
